title: Race-lap query ranking head over simultaneous pit candidates
summary: Pit calls are relative decisions among drivers on the same lap, not independent Bernoulli events.
rationale: Pit calls are relative decisions among drivers on the same lap, not independent Bernoulli events. XGBoost rankers are designed for grouped queries and pairwise/listwise ordering, which matches choosing who is most pit-ready within a `(Year,Race,LapNumber)` snapshot.
implementation_hint: Train an `XGBRanker` with `qid=(Year,Race,LapNumber)` or a narrower candidate set such as only drivers not in immediate cooldown. Use labels 1 for `PitNextLap`, 0 otherwise. Blend the rank score with the binary model, or use it only as a meta-feature. Consider separate rankers for dry and wet compounds.
expected_effect: Sharper within-lap ordering and fewer false positives on laps where only one or two cars are realistic pit candidates.
risk: Many race-lap groups may contain no positives; filter groups or upweight groups with at least one positive to avoid wasting pairwise signal.
